title: Your Care Journey Roadmap
intro: Every family's path looks different. Tell us what's happening right now, and we'll put together a starting roadmap — priorities, first steps, and who to involve.
prompt: What is happening in your family right now?

stages.label: Parent is independent, but concerns are emerging
stages.title: Roadmap: Getting Ahead of Emerging Concerns

sections.heading: Immediate priorities
items: Have an honest, low-pressure conversation with your parent
Start noting specific changes you've observed, with dates

sections.heading: Next 7 days
items: Take the Care Readiness Assessment together if they're open to it
Do a quick home safety walk-through

sections.heading: Next 30 days
items: Schedule a check-up with their doctor
Start a simple family caregiving plan

sections.heading: Documents to locate
items: Power of attorney (if it exists)
A current medication list

sections.heading: People to involve
items: Siblings or other close family
Their primary care doctor

sections.heading: Immediate priorities
items: Map out what daily support is actually needed
Identify who's currently providing it, and where the gaps are

sections.heading: Next 7 days
items: Use the Family Responsibility Planner to divide tasks
Set up a shared calendar or list for appointments and meds

sections.heading: Next 30 days
items: Look into respite care options for the primary caregiver
Revisit the plan once routines settle in

sections.heading: Documents to locate
items: Insurance cards and policy numbers
Financial power of attorney

sections.heading: People to involve
items: All siblings, even distant ones
A home care agency, if help is needed

sections.heading: Immediate priorities
items: Identify one point of contact for the care team
Get a current medication list to the hospital

sections.heading: Next 7 days
items: Attend daily rounds if possible
Start asking about the discharge plan early

sections.heading: Next 30 days
items: Prepare the home for their return
Schedule the first follow-up appointment

sections.heading: Documents to locate
items: Health care proxy / medical POA
Insurance information

sections.heading: People to involve
items: Hospital social worker or discharge planner
Family members who can help post-discharge

sections.heading: Immediate priorities
items: Clarify budget and funding sources
List must-haves versus nice-to-haves for a community

sections.heading: Next 7 days
items: Use the Assisted Living Comparison tool
Schedule 2–3 tours

sections.heading: Next 30 days
items: Review contracts carefully before signing
Plan the emotional and logistical side of moving day

sections.heading: Documents to locate
items: Financial records and any long-term care insurance
Power of attorney

sections.heading: People to involve
items: Your parent, in every decision possible
A financial advisor or elder law attorney

stages.label: Loved one is receiving hospice care
stages.title: Roadmap: Hospice Care

sections.heading: Immediate priorities
items: Understand what hospice does and doesn't cover
Get the 24/7 support number saved somewhere visible

sections.heading: Next 7 days
items: Ask the hospice team what to expect day-to-day
Loop in family members who want to be involved

sections.heading: Next 30 days
items: Lean on the social worker and chaplain for support
Take care of your own rest and wellbeing

sections.heading: Documents to locate
items: Advance directives
Funeral or final wishes, if documented

sections.heading: People to involve
items: The hospice care team
Close family and anyone who needs to say goodbye

stages.label: A loved one recently died
stages.title: Roadmap: The First Steps After a Loss

sections.heading: Immediate priorities
items: There's no need to handle everything today
Contact close family and the funeral home

sections.heading: Next 7 days
items: Request 10+ copies of the death certificate
Start the After-a-Loss Task Manager

sections.heading: Next 30 days
items: Notify Social Security, banks, and insurance
Begin gathering estate documents

sections.heading: Documents to locate
items: Will and estate documents
Life insurance policies

sections.heading: People to involve
items: An estate attorney, if needed
Grief support — for you, too

caveat: This reflects one reasonable path for your situation — not the only path. Adjust anything that doesn't fit.
